DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Jessica Dayton of ADZ Law LLP, Tel. No. 1-650-458-2300 appearing for tye Plaintiff;
Atty. Lauren R. Presser of Tyson & Mendes, Tel. No. 1-858-999-0427 appearing of the defendants Uber Ech & Rasier LLC;
Atty. Morgan H. Piercy of the Bremer Whyte LLP, Tel. No. 1-949-221-1000 appearing for the defendant Galvez-Cortes

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Gaspar Cortes' demurrer to the second, third, fourth, fifth, and seventh causes of action for negligence per se, assault, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress and gender violence per Civil Code 52.4 in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jane Doe is overruled in its entirety. The factual disputes raised by Mr. Cortes in his opposition are not amenable to resolution on a demurrer. Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts for a claim of negligence per se and Penal Code 273 requires a general intent to commit the act of inflicting unjustified suffering on a child. Plaintiff has also alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for assault. Mr. Cortes' alleged conduct of driving the car to the side of the highway, his invitation to plaintiff to sit in the front seat, his pointing to his crotch while plaintiff was seated next to him and his lewd comments supports an inference that the natural and probable consequence of his conduct would result in an offensive touching. Plaintiff has also alleged enough facts to support a claim for false imprisonment because a confinement against one's will can be effectuated by words, gestures or acts and plaintiff could reasonably perceive an implied threat of force in Mr. Cortes' conduct. The alleged facts are also outrageous enough to support an IIED claim. Because assault is a crime that requires a threat of offensive touching, plaintiff has also alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for violation of section 52.4. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar May-15-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Atty. Jessica Dayton of ADZ Law LLP, Tel. No. 1-650-458-2300 appearing for tye Plaintiff;
Atty. Lauren R. Presser of Tyson & Mendes, Tel. No. 1-858-999-0427 appearing of the defendants Uber Ech & Rasier LLC;
Atty. Morgan H. Piercy of the Bremer Whyte LLP, Tel. No. 1-949-221-1000 appearing for the defendant Galvez-Cortes.

RULING - Defendants Uber Technologies, Inc. and Rasier, LLC's demurrer to the third, fourth, fifth and seventh causes of action assault, false imprisonment, intentional infliction of emotional distress and gender violence per Civil Code 52.4 in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Jane Doe - Sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for violation of section 52.4 and overruled as to the third, fourth and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff's allegations are sufficient to support a finding that Uber was an employer of Mr. Cortes and Mr. Cortes was acting in the course and scope of that employment. (Doe v. Uber Technologies, Inc. (ND CA 2016) 184 F. Supp. 3d 774; see also Dynamex Operations West, Inc. v. Superior Court (April 30, 2018) 2018 DAR 3856 (very recent California Supreme Court case suggesting that a company whose business is to drive vehicles such as Uber is the employer of its drivers, at least for some purposes)). Plaintiff has also alleged sufficient facts to support a claim for assault. Mr. Cortes' alleged conduct of driving the car to the side of the highway, his invitation to plaintiff to sit in the front seat, his pointing to his crotch while plaintiff was seated next to him, and his lewd comments supports an inference that the natural and probable consequence of his conduct would result in an offensive touching. Plaintiff has also alleged enough facts to support a claim for false imprisonment because a confinement against one's will can be effectuated by words, gestures or acts and plaintiff could reasonably perceive an implied threat of force in Mr. Cortes' conduct. Uber and Rasier are not liable for any alleged violation of section. 52.4 because there is no allegation that they personally committed the violence proscribed by that section, nor is it reasonably possible that plaintiff could make such an allegation. Parties to meet and confer regarding form of order. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR #8600 =302/hk
